players.name: Mouhib Chamakh
players.bio: Young Club Africain stopper making his World Cup bow with just 3 caps to his name. At 24, Chamakh is the understudy with everything to prove on the grandest stage. Big gloves to fill, big dreams to match.
players.facts: Shares a surname with Morocco legend Marouane Chamakh — no relation, but the name alone carries weight.

players.name: Ali Abdi
players.bio: Sfax-born left-back who found his groove in French football with Nice. A veteran of 46 caps and 7 international goals — impressive numbers for a defender. Ali Abdi brings experience, energy and a mean left foot to the backline.
players.facts: 7 international goals from left-back puts him ahead of many strikers in the Tunisia squad. Overachiever alert.

players.name: Montassar Talbi
players.bio: Paris-born centre-back who chose the Eagles over Les Bleus. With 64 caps, Talbi is Tunisia's defensive cornerstone — composed, commanding and tough to beat in the air. Lorient's loss is Tunisia's considerable gain.
players.facts: Born in Paris but flew the Tunisian flag — one of several Franco-Tunisians in this squad making North Africa proud.

players.name: Omar Rekik
players.bio: Born in the Netherlands, raised on Tunisian pride. Omar Rekik is a composed young centre-back currently plying his trade in Slovenia with Maribor. Only 6 caps so far — World Cup 2026 could be his breakthrough moment.
players.facts: Born in Helmond, Netherlands — part of a new generation of diaspora players reshaping Tunisian football's identity.

players.name: Adem Arous
players.bio: Born in Saudi Arabia, playing in Turkey, representing Tunisia — Adem Arous has lived a life already at just 21. The Kasımpaşa centre-back is the youngest defender in the squad and heading to his first World Cup.
players.facts: Born in Medina, Saudi Arabia — making him one of the most internationally-born players in the entire tournament.

players.name: Dylan Bronn
players.bio: Cannes-born, Swiss-based stopper with over 50 caps for the Eagles. Dylan Bronn is the seasoned rock of Tunisia's defence — composed under pressure and rarely flustered. Servette's gain is also very much Tunisia's.
players.facts: With 52 caps, Bronn has been capped more times than several players who've played in two World Cups already.

players.name: Elias Achouri
players.full_name: Mohamed Elias Achouri; مُحَمَّد إِلْيَاس عَاشُورِيّ
players.bio: Born on the island of Réunion and now starring for FC Copenhagen, Achouri is a tricky left winger who thrives on direct running and creative flair. His 30 caps and 4 goals show he punches above his island roots.
players.facts: Born on Réunion Island in the Indian Ocean — the most geographically unique birthplace of any player in the Tunisia squad.

players.name: Elias Saad
players.bio: Hamburg-born winger who brings Bundesliga grit to Tunisia's attack. Elias Saad has hit 4 international goals from 15 caps — a fine return for a wide player. Hannover 96's creative spark lights up the left flank.
players.facts: Born in Hamburg, played in Germany, representing Tunisia — the classic diaspora journey done with flair and goals.

players.name: Hazem Mastouri
players.bio: Tunis-born striker who found an unlikely home at Dynamo Makhachkala in Russia's top flight. Mastouri is a physical, hard-working centre-forward with 4 goals from 19 caps. Doesn't take the obvious path — on or off the pitch.
players.facts: Plays club football in Makhachkala, Russia — making him one of the more geographically adventurous strikers at the tournament.

players.name: Hannibal Mejbri
players.bio: The jewel of Tunisian football. Born in France, raised at Manchester United, now starring for Burnley — Hannibal Mejbri is passionate, technically gifted and box-office. At just 23, the number 10 shirt fits perfectly.
players.facts: Named after the legendary Carthaginian general Hannibal — no pressure wearing that shirt at a World Cup, then.

players.name: Ismaël Gharbi
players.full_name: Ismaël Seifallah Gharbi, إسماعيل سيفالله غربي
players.bio: Paris-born attacking midfielder who came through PSG's academy before finding his feet in the Bundesliga with Augsburg. Gharbi is creative, quick-thinking and technically slick — a dangerous option off the bench or in the XI.
players.facts: A PSG academy graduate playing at the World Cup for Tunisia — the ultimate 'one that got away' for French football scouts.

players.name: Mortadha Ben Ouanes
players.bio: Sousse-born winger who has been a reliable squad member across multiple campaigns. Ben Ouanes brings domestic experience from the Turkish league and 18 caps of international know-how to Tunisia's attacking options.
players.facts: At 31, Ben Ouanes is the oldest outfield attacker in the Tunisia squad — proof experience never goes out of style.

players.name: Rani Khedira
players.bio: Yes, he is indeed the brother of Sami Khedira, World Cup winner with Germany in 2014. Rani chose Tunisia and brings Bundesliga quality from Union Berlin. Only 3 caps but a surname that needs no introduction.
players.facts: His brother Sami won the World Cup with Germany in 2014. Rani chose a different path — and a very different squad.

players.name: Khalil Ayari
players.bio: Teenager turning heads at Paris Saint-Germain — no small feat. Khalil Ayari is one of the most exciting young midfielders in Tunisian football, with 4 caps already at just 21. The future is very much now for this PSG starlet.
players.facts: Playing first-team football at PSG at 21 while representing Tunisia internationally — quietly one of the tournament's great prospects.

players.name: Hadj Mahmoud
players.bio: Composed central midfielder currently making his mark with Lugano in Switzerland. Hadj Mahmoud has earned 9 caps and offers Tunisia a tidy, disciplined presence in the engine room. Under-the-radar but increasingly important.
players.facts: Plays his club football in Switzerland's Super League — one of the quieter leagues producing World Cup players in 2026.

players.name: Aymen Dahmen
players.bio: Sfaxien's reliable shot-stopper and Tunisia's number two with 37 caps to his name. Dahmen is an experienced, agile goalkeeper who has kept the Eagles' net on numerous occasions. A trusted pair of hands behind the scenes.
players.facts: With 37 caps as a backup keeper, Dahmen has more international appearances than some starting goalkeepers at this tournament.

players.name: Ellyes Skhiri
players.bio: Tunisia's midfield engine and most-capped outfield player in this squad at 83. Skhiri is the heartbeat at Eintracht Frankfurt — tireless, technically sound and a leader in every sense. The Eagles simply do not fly without him.
players.facts: With 83 caps, Skhiri has played more games for Tunisia than some countries have played in their entire World Cup history.

players.name: Rayan Elloumi
players.bio: Born in 2007 and already at the World Cup — Rayan Elloumi is the tournament's youngest Eagle. The Vancouver Whitecaps forward is a MLS-forged teenager with raw talent and absolutely nothing to lose on this stage.
players.facts: Born in 2007, making him one of the youngest players at the entire 2026 World Cup. He was in primary school when Tunisia last qualified.

players.name: Firas Chaouat
players.bio: Club Africain's local hero and a consistent finisher for Tunisia with 6 goals from 30 caps. Chaouat is a streetwise, experienced forward who knows the Tunisian game inside out. Domestic football's finest ambassador at this World Cup.
players.facts: One of only two Club Africain players in the squad — representing Tunisia's domestic league on the world's biggest stage.

players.name: Yan Valery
players.bio: Versatile right-sided defender born in France who has become a dependable international for Tunisia. Now at Young Boys in Switzerland, Valery brings pace, positional intelligence and 22 caps of experience to the backline.
players.facts: Previously played in the Premier League with Southampton — swapped English rain for Swiss precision without missing a beat.

players.name: Mohamed Amine Ben Hamida
players.bio: Espérance de Tunis defender who is the squad's homegrown backbone. Ben Hamida has earned 13 caps representing the club football culture of Tunis on the international stage. Reliable, committed and proudly Tunisian.
players.facts: One of the few players in the squad who plays his club football in Tunisia itself — a true local hero at a global tournament.

players.name: Sabri Ben Hessen
players.bio: Étoile du Sahel's guardian and Tunisia's third-choice keeper heading into the tournament. Ben Hessen may be the last line of defence between the sticks but his presence keeps the squad's goalkeeping depth sharp and competitive.
players.facts: Third-choice keeper at a World Cup — statistically the least likely to play, yet still one of only 26 people on the planet here.

players.name: Moutaz Neffati
players.bio: Young right-back or winger currently developing with IFK Norrköping in Sweden. At just 21 with 5 caps, Neffati is one of the fresher faces in the squad — a Sweden-polished talent ready to make his mark internationally.
players.facts: Plies his trade in Sweden's Allsvenskan — the Nordic league quietly producing more African internationals than you'd expect.

players.name: Raed Chikhaoui
players.bio: US Monastir's promising young defender making his World Cup squad at just 22 — and without a senior cap to his name. Chikhaoui is a bold selection but Tunisia clearly believe in the talent. Zero caps, maximum occasion.
players.facts: Arrives at the World Cup with zero senior caps — making him one of the most uncapped players at the entire tournament.

players.name: Anis Ben Slimane
players.bio: Creative midfielder currently at Norwich City with 41 caps and an eye for goal. Ben Slimane is a technically gifted, progressive passer who can unlock defences. A Championship-hardened talent ready for World Cup football.
players.facts: Has more international caps (41) than some players who've represented their nations at multiple World Cups. Quietly prolific.

players.name: Sebastian Tounekti
players.bio: Celtic's Tunisia international brings Scottish Premiership steel to the Eagles' midfield. Tounekti is a dynamic, box-to-box midfielder with 12 caps and a taste for the big occasion — Glasgow's loss is Tunisia's gain this summer.
players.facts: Wins his football in Glasgow's pressure-cooker Old Firm atmosphere — a World Cup crowd holds no fears for this Celtic man.
